# Project Charter

<!-- Generated by `spec-kitty charter generate` -->

Generated: 2026-05-01T10:25:11Z

## Testing Standards

- Every component requires a Storybook story covering at minimum: default state, all interactive states (hover, focus, active, disabled), and responsive breakpoints. Automated WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility scans via axe-core (or Storybook a11y addon) are required before a work package is marked done. Visual diff against the Claude Design reference screenshots (tmp/) is required for new and modified components. Cross-browser smoke tests (Chrome, Firefox, Safari latest) are required before a release tag. No minimum line-coverage threshold — visual and accessibility conformance is the primary quality bar.


## Quality Gates

- A component is done when: (1) Storybook story renders without console errors; (2) axe-core reports zero WCAG 2.1 AA violations; (3) visual review against the reference screenshots is approved; (4) the component documents its token dependencies (which --sk-* variables it uses). CSS/SCSS output must use only --sk-* tokens — no hardcoded color, spacing, or type values outside the token file. Conventional commits enforced via commitlint. One maintainer approval required for PRs touching component files or the core token layer; documentation-only PRs (Storybook stories, README) may self-merge if CI passes.


## Performance Benchmarks

- Base CSS token file (colors_and_type.css) must remain under 20 KB uncompressed. Full Angular component bundle under 150 KB compressed per component chunk. Storybook CI build time under 3 minutes. No runtime performance targets beyond standard browser rendering — these are static/presentational components, not application logic.


## Branch Strategy

- PRs must include a screenshot or visual diff for any component change. Token additions or renames require maintainer sign-off. Storybook build must pass in CI before merge. One human approval required for any change to the --sk-* token namespace (colors_and_type.scss or equivalent). Exception: documentation-only changes (stories, README, SKILL.md) can self-merge when CI is green.

- Deployment constraints: Versioned with semantic versioning (semver). Token file and component distributions published to npm under the @spec-kitty scope; plain HTML/CSS layer optionally mirrored to a CDN. The Claude Code skill (SKILL.md) and doctrine elements (YAML + prose) are distributed as files within the published package, not as a separate registry. Breaking changes to --sk-* token names require a major version bump. Additive framework ports (e.g., Vue, Svelte) may be added as sub-packages without a major bump. tmp/ is gitignored and not distributed.


## Governance Activation

```yaml
mission: software-dev
selected_paradigms: [atomic-design]
selected_directives: [DIRECTIVE_001, DIRECTIVE_003, DIRECTIVE_010, DIRECTIVE_031, DIRECTIVE_032, DIRECTIVE_037]
selected_tactics: []
available_tools: [git, spec-kitty]
template_set: software-dev-default
```

## Policy Summary

- Intent: A reusable, multi-framework design system for the Spec Kitty ecosystem. Publishes SCSS/CSS custom-property tokens, HTML primitives, JavaScript utilities, Angular components, and docsite theme elements as importable artifacts consumed by the SK operator dashboard, public documentation site, blog surfaces, marketing pages, and slidedecks. Also ships pluggable doctrine elements — YAML + prose brand-voice and visual-style guidelines — for use inside agentic workflows (Claude Code skill, charter voice policy). Primary v1 consumers: SK dashboard UI overhaul (issue #650) and docsite refresh (issue #648). The Claude Design reference set (tmp/) is the authoritative visual baseline for v1.

- Languages/Frameworks: SCSS for design tokens and base styles; vanilla HTML for primitive component markup; JavaScript (ES modules) for interactive component behavior; Angular for the primary framework-specific component library; Storybook as the component documentation and visual-regression baseline. CSS custom properties (--sk-* namespace) are the single authoritative token distribution format consumed across all framework targets. Secondary: docsite theme integration (static site generator TBD). No unit-test framework — quality signal is visual conformance and accessibility audit, not line coverage.

- Testing: Every component requires a Storybook story covering at minimum: default state, all interactive states (hover, focus, active, disabled), and responsive breakpoints. Automated WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility scans via axe-core (or Storybook a11y addon) are required before a work package is marked done. Visual diff against the Claude Design reference screenshots (tmp/) is required for new and modified components. Cross-browser smoke tests (Chrome, Firefox, Safari latest) are required before a release tag. No minimum line-coverage threshold — visual and accessibility conformance is the primary quality bar.

- Quality Gates: A component is done when: (1) Storybook story renders without console errors; (2) axe-core reports zero WCAG 2.1 AA violations; (3) visual review against the reference screenshots is approved; (4) the component documents its token dependencies (which --sk-* variables it uses). CSS/SCSS output must use only --sk-* tokens — no hardcoded color, spacing, or type values outside the token file. Conventional commits enforced via commitlint. One maintainer approval required for PRs touching component files or the core token layer; documentation-only PRs (Storybook stories, README) may self-merge if CI passes.

- Review Policy: PRs must include a screenshot or visual diff for any component change. Token additions or renames require maintainer sign-off. Storybook build must pass in CI before merge. One human approval required for any change to the --sk-* token namespace (colors_and_type.scss or equivalent). Exception: documentation-only changes (stories, README, SKILL.md) can self-merge when CI is green.

- Performance Targets: Base CSS token file (colors_and_type.css) must remain under 20 KB uncompressed. Full Angular component bundle under 150 KB compressed per component chunk. Storybook CI build time under 3 minutes. No runtime performance targets beyond standard browser rendering — these are static/presentational components, not application logic.

- Deployment Constraints: Versioned with semantic versioning (semver). Token file and component distributions published to npm under the @spec-kitty scope; plain HTML/CSS layer optionally mirrored to a CDN. The Claude Code skill (SKILL.md) and doctrine elements (YAML + prose) are distributed as files within the published package, not as a separate registry. Breaking changes to --sk-* token names require a major version bump. Additive framework ports (e.g., Vue, Svelte) may be added as sub-packages without a major bump. tmp/ is gitignored and not distributed.


## Project Directives

1. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_001` to planning and implementation decisions.
2. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_003` to planning and implementation decisions.
3. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_010` to planning and implementation decisions.
4. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_031` to planning and implementation decisions.
5. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_032` to planning and implementation decisions.
6. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_037` to planning and implementation decisions.
7. Respect risk boundaries: Highest-cost failure: brand drift — components or token values that diverge from the Claude Design reference, causing visual inconsistency across consuming repos. Second: accessibility regression — a published component with WCAG 2.1 AA violations shipped to a public-facing surface. Third: versioning incompatibility — a token rename that silently breaks consuming repos without a major version bump. Privacy and security risk is minimal (static assets, no user data, no auth). Breaking changes to --sk-* token names are blocked without a major version increment and must be flagged prominently in CHANGELOG.

8. Keep documentation synchronized with workflow and behavior changes.

| `DIRECTIVE:DIRECTIVE_031` | directive | Every design decision must be made with explicit awareness of the bounded context it belongs to. Code, terminology, and abstractions must align with the context's ubiquitous language. Crossing a context boundary requires an explicit translation layer; implicit coupling across boundaries is prohibited.
 | `_LIBRARY/directive-directive-031.md` |
| `DIRECTIVE:DIRECTIVE_032` | directive | Agents must never assume that shared vocabulary implies shared meaning. Before acting on any task that contains domain-specific, ambiguous, or overloaded terminology, the agent must explicitly state its interpretation of each key term and confirm that interpretation with the requester. Misaligned understanding discovered after implementation is far more costly than a brief clarification exchange before it begins.
 | `_LIBRARY/directive-directive-032.md` |
| `DIRECTIVE:DIRECTIVE_037` | directive | Behavior-describing artifacts must evolve together. When observable behavior changes, the canonical examples, acceptance checks, narrative specifications, user documentation, glossary terms, code-level documentation, and architecture records must be updated in the same change so no reader is left with a stale picture of the system.
 | `_LIBRARY/directive-directive-037.md` |

| `TACTIC:atdd-adversarial-acceptance` | tactic | Strengthen acceptance criteria by deliberately exploring how a feature could fail in real usage, then converting selected failure scenarios into adversarial acceptance tests that define reject, constrain, or degrade boundaries. | `_LIBRARY/tactic-atdd-adversarial-acceptance.md` |

 | `_LIBRARY/tactic-connascence-analysis.md` |

Identifies where assumptions are embedded in the wrong places, flags premature abstraction, and recommends minimal structural investments that preserve adaptability without over-engineering.
 | `_LIBRARY/tactic-easy-to-change.md` |

| `TACTIC:input-validation-fail-fast` | tactic | Validate input data comprehensively before processing to avoid wasting resources on invalid data. Provide clear, actionable feedback on errors while protecting system internals from exposure. | `_LIBRARY/tactic-input-validation-fail-fast.md` |
| `TACTIC:language-driven-design` | tactic | Treat language drift as an architectural signal. When terminology becomes inconsistent or ambiguous across code, docs, and conversation, it predicts deeper structural problems. Reviewers use linguistic checks to catch architectural issues before they manifest as bugs or coupling.
 | `_LIBRARY/tactic-language-driven-design.md` |
| `TACTIC:premortem-risk-identification` | tactic | Proactively surface failure modes for a plan or decision by deliberately attempting to sabotage it before execution. | `_LIBRARY/tactic-premortem-risk-identification.md` |
| `TACTIC:quality-gate-verification` | tactic | Verify the relevant test and static validation gates for a change before review handoff and distinguish introduced failures from pre-existing debt. | `_LIBRARY/tactic-quality-gate-verification.md` |
| `TACTIC:tdd-red-green-refactor` | tactic | Enforce short, verifiable coding loops that improve design while keeping behavior safe. | `_LIBRARY/tactic-tdd-red-green-refactor.md` |
| `TACTIC:test-boundaries-by-responsibility` | tactic | Determine test scope by identifying which components are directly responsible for the behavior under test, rather than following structural layer boundaries. Reduces brittle tests while ensuring meaningful validation. | `_LIBRARY/tactic-test-boundaries-by-responsibility.md` |
| `TACTIC:test-pyramid-progression` | tactic | Run tests bottom-up through pyramid layers to get fast feedback and avoid wasting time on slow tests when fast tests fail. The execution order mirrors the ROI order: the cheapest, fastest tests run first because they catch the most bugs per second of CI time.
 | `_LIBRARY/tactic-test-pyramid-progression.md` |
| `TACTIC:testing-select-appropriate-level` | tactic | Choose the right test level (unit, integration, system) for a change based on scope of impact, risk of failure, and feedback cost. Avoids both over-testing low-risk changes and under-testing high-risk ones. Grounded in the test pyramid model where ROI decreases as test scope widens.
 | `_LIBRARY/tactic-testing-select-appropriate-level.md` |
| `TACTIC:usage-examples-sync` | tactic | Keep canonical usage examples, acceptance checks, and the narrative artifacts that quote or reference them in step with behavior changes so no downstream reader is left with stale examples.
 | `_LIBRARY/tactic-usage-examples-sync.md` |
| `STYLEGUIDE:kitty-glossary-writing` | styleguide | Clear, not clever: definitions exist to help the reader understand, not to demonstrate expertise. | `_LIBRARY/styleguide-kitty-glossary-writing.md` |
| `STYLEGUIDE:python-conventions` | styleguide | Guard clauses over nesting: validate inputs early and fail fast rather than wrapping logic in conditional branches. | `_LIBRARY/styleguide-python-conventions.md` |

## Amendment Process

Charter and governance amendments via PR with rationale comment; no special approval beyond the standard review policy. Changes to the atomic design hierarchy classification or the core token naming convention require maintainer sign-off and an ADR entry. The Claude Design reference set (tmp/) is the fixed visual baseline for v1 — token values derived from it cannot be overridden by individual PRs without producing a new design reference first.


## Exception Policy

Components may be added under an @experimental tag in Storybook without passing the full quality gate, provided they are clearly marked and excluded from the stable package export. Framework ports by community contributors (Vue, Svelte, etc.) do not require maintainer sign-off provided they pass CI and visually match the reference Storybook story. Accessibility waivers for known browser-limitation issues may be noted in the component story with a tracking issue — they do not block release but must be documented.
